response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced generalist software engineer with strong full-stack, testing, and open-source credentials, but he does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is minimal and dated (a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a thesis project), not the mandated 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He lists SQL/MySQL/MongoDB but no SQL Server or Oracle depth, and he has no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience — RIDE Capital was a retail investment product built in Node/TypeScript, not investment banking. The optional skills he does cover (CI/CD, scripting) cannot compensate for the missing mandatory core.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Proven communicator — workshops, talks, and courses demonstrate strong verbal/written skills
concerns: No substantial or recent C++ experience, and none on the Windows platform
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain background
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work centers on MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: No capital-markets or fixed-income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and communication skills
